/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/research/G.problem: Internal contradiction: claims "BENE/the 0.1.0 predecessor's edges over KAOS are the temporal/ durable runtime, storage/ protocol (sqlite+postgres), runtime/ abstraction, intake.py, benchmarks namespace, and a 37-tool MCP server" — but three of the six are contradicted by this same document: (1) KAOS has kaos/intake.py with the identical capability (its own KAOS inventory line and subsumption row 24, which even adds --intake-only preview); (2) bene/benchmarks/ is called an "empty shell"/docstring-only placeholder in BENE shortcoming 13, while KAOS ships metaharness/benchmarks + 13 demo_*_bench suites (row 30); (3) a 37-tool MCP server is not an edge over KAOS's 58 tools, stated one bullet earlier. An interviewer catches this within the same page.
/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/research/G.fix: Replace with: "BENE/the 0.1.0 predecessor's edges over KAOS are the temporal/ durable runtime, the storage/ protocol (sqlite+postgres), and the runtime/ abstraction — while KAOS holds exactly the surfaces BENE lacks (eval probes w/ falsify/verify, dream consolidation, experiment journal, failure diagnose/lookup store, ideal-state artifacts, systemic alerts)."

/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/BEN.problem: "`bene.yaml` gains a `kernel:` section (enabled, autonomy defaults, consolidation schedule)" — factually wrong: no kernel: section exists in bene.yaml or bene.yaml.example, and no code anywhere reads a kernel config key (grep for kernel config consumption across bene/ returns nothing). Also `bene init` does not create v2 tables — they are created lazily by kernel CLI commands (_kernel_stores → ensure_v2) or attach_kernel, so "kernel on for new dbs" only holds on first kernel use. This gap is not tracked in CLAIMS-AUDIT either (no yaml/config row), so it is an unacknowledged false claim.
/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/BEN.fix: Replace with: "Kernel tables are created lazily on first kernel command or `attach_kernel` (sane defaults, no config required); a `bene.yaml` `kernel:` section (autonomy defaults, consolidation schedule) is planned — see CLAIMS-AUDIT." And add the corresponding planned row to CLAIMS-AUDIT.

/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/BEN.problem: "No global 'the agent is good' score — trust is per-domain composite" and D8's "trust summaries are *per capability domain*" overstate the code. bene/kernel/trust.py summary()/eligible() accept domain="*" but never filter by it — all four signals and the composite are computed globally per agent and the domain string is merely echoed back in the result dict. What exists per-domain is capability-level autonomy requirements in the registry, not per-domain trust scores. A reader of trust.py catches the no-op parameter.
/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/BEN.fix: Soften both: §6 bullet → "No opaque single score — trust is a per-agent composite with four visible component signals; per-domain breakdown is reserved (domain parameter present, filtering planned)". D8 → "trust summaries expose an explicit composite with components; per-capability-domain breakdown is the planned refinement (autonomy levels are already per-capability)". Or implement domain filtering in summary()/eligible().

/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/KER.problem: 规格说 v2 版本由『existing schema_version 表中的一行 kernel_schema_version row』追踪，且声称 "7 CREATE TABLE (6 regular + 1 virtual)"。实现（bene/kernel/schema_v2.py:100-103）建的是独立的 kernel_schema_version 表，遗留 schema_version 完全不碰——共 8 个 CREATE TABLE（7 regular + 1 virtual）。该已知偏差只写在 schema_v2.py docstring 里，违反规格自己第 3 行的『偏差须同 commit 回写本文件』规则；对照读 spec 与 sqlite_master 立刻能抓出。
/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/KER.fix: 8 CREATE TABLE (7 regular + 1 virtual). **No ALTER on legacy tables, ever.** Migration guard: `ensure_v2(conn)` is idempotent; a dedicated `kernel_schema_version` table (separate from the legacy `schema_version`, which has no component column and is never touched) tracks v2 revisions.

/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/KER.problem: 规格 DDL 是 contentless FTS（content='', contentless_delete=1）；实现（schema_v2.py:52-54）是带 engram_id UNINDEXED 列的普通 fts5 表（search 需要 JOIN engram_id，contentless 表做不到）。已知偏差但规格 DDL 未更新。
/home/admin/gh/bene-main/docs/design/KER.fix: 把 DDL 块替换为实现版本：CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS engram_fts USING fts5(\n    engram_id UNINDEXED, title, body, tokenize='porter'\n);  -- engram_id stored UNINDEXED (not contentless) so search can JOIN back to engrams
